name: How long do I have to file for occupational illness comp in Utah after diagnosis?
description: Report within 180 days, file claim within 1 year of diagnosis/disability under Utah ODA rules.[6][4]

mainEntity.name: How long do I have to file for occupational illness comp in Utah after diagnosis?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report within 180 days, file claim within 1 year of diagnosis/disability under Utah ODA rules.[6][4]
acceptedAnswer.description: You generally have one year from the date of injury or diagnosis to file, but for occupational diseases under Utah's ODA, it may hinge on when disability occurs.[4][6] Utah law sets a 180-day reporting window to your supervisor, with stricter claim filing deadlines via the Labor Commission.[6] Key factors: date symptoms became disabling, continuous exposure proof, and no-fault requirements under ODA exclusivity.[1] Track all dates meticulously, submit medical reports promptly, and appeal denials within 30 days. Delays often lead to outright rejection.
